title: National Right to Life Endorses Greg McCortney, the Senator Who Kept Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
author: James Silberman
orientation: Silberman charges that National Right to Life endorsed Oklahoma State Senator Greg McCortney, the committee chair who killed SB13 (the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act), giving him political cover against abolitionist challenger Carisa Roberson. The piece argues this is part of a recurring pattern in which pro-life establishment groups reward politicians who block immediate abolition.
audiences: donors to National Right to Life and state affiliates
pro-life Christians weighing abolition vs. incrementalism
candidates and campaign staff running on abolition

questions.question: What bill did Greg McCortney prevent from receiving a hearing, and what would it have done?
questions.answer: McCortney, as committee chairman, denied a hearing to SB13, the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act, which would have abolished abortion in the state, declared preborn humans equal under the law, and voided any government decree violating the right to life.
questions.quote: The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act (SB13) would have abolished abortion in the State of Oklahoma. It would have established that preborn humans are equal under the law and that any government decree violating the unalienable right to life was void.

questions.question: Why does the article say National Right to Life's endorsement of McCortney is contradictory?
questions.audience: donors to National Right to Life and state affiliates
questions.answer: Because the same organization lobbied against SB13 and then endorsed the very senator most responsible for killing it, effectively rewarding him for keeping abortion legal in Oklahoma.
questions.quote: National Right to Life, after lobbying hard against the bill that would've abolished abortion, has endorsed the man most responsible for killing the bill.

questions.question: Who specifically lobbied against SB13 on behalf of National Right to Life?
questions.answer: Tony Lauinger, Vice President of National Right to Life and President of Oklahomans for Life, has lobbied against the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act every year since it was introduced in 2016.
questions.quote: Tony Lauinger, the Vice President of National Right to Life and the President of Oklahomans for Life, lobbied against the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act, as he has every year since it was introduced in 2016.

questions.question: Who is challenging McCortney and on what platform?
questions.answer: Carisa Roberson, an abolitionist, is challenging McCortney on a platform of supporting the very bill (SB13) that he prevented from being heard.
questions.quote: McCortney is up for reelection this year and facing a challenge from Carisa Roberson, an abolitionist who is running on a platform of supporting the bill that McCortney prevented from being heard.

questions.question: How does Silberman describe the political function a pro-life endorsement plays for a politician like McCortney?
questions.audience: pro-life Christians weighing abolition vs. incrementalism
questions.answer: He argues the endorsement releases pressure on a politician who refuses to abolish abortion in a state that wants it abolished, allowing him to claim a pro-life label while undercutting the abolitionist running against him.
questions.quote: But that pressure is released if a pro-life organization comes along and justifies your actions. Then you get to say you're a pro-life™-approved candidate and the person running against you on a platform to abolish abortion has their legs cut out from underneath them.

questions.question: What evidence does the article cite that Oklahomans broadly want abortion abolished?
questions.audience: pro-life Christians weighing abolition vs. incrementalism
questions.answer: Silberman points to thousands attending Abolition Day at the Capitol, the Oklahoma Republican Party platform calling for an abolition bill, the Oklahoma Baptists' formal resolution, and the Oklahoma Free Will Baptist platform.
questions.quote: Oklahoma Baptists have [formally requested](https://freethestates.org/2019/11/oklahoma-baptists-resolution/index.html) abortion's total an immediate abolition “without exception or compromise.” The Oklahoma Free Will Baptist platform formally demands abortion's abolition and the rejection of pro-life compromise.

questions.question: What parallel does the article draw to events in Texas?
questions.answer: It cites Texas House committee chair Jeff Leach, who killed the Abolition of Abortion in Texas Act and was then given a Courageous Defense of Life award by Texas Alliance for Life, as another instance of pro-life groups rewarding obstructionist politicians.
questions.quote: The Abolition of Abortion in Texas Act was [denied a committee vote](https://christiannews.net/2019/04/10/texas-chairman-kills-bill-to-outlaw-abortion-for-criminalizing-women-involved-in-murdering-their-babies/) by House Judiciary and Civil Jurisprudence committee chair Jeff Leach. Just a few months later, Texas Alliance for Life [awarded Leach](https://www.facebook.com/search/top/?q=texas%20alliance%20for%20life%20jeff%20leach%20couragoues&epa=SEARCH_BOX) with their Courageous Defense of Life award.

questions.question: According to the article, what is the only thing keeping abortion legal in Oklahoma for the foreseeable future?
questions.audience: donors to National Right to Life and state affiliates
questions.answer: Cover from establishment pro-life organizations; without that political insulation, voter outrage would end the careers of politicians who block abolition and they would be replaced by abolitionists.
questions.quote: The only way abortion remains legal in Oklahoma for the foreseeable future is if the politicians who keep abortion legal receive cover from the establishment pro-life organizations. And that's exactly what they're getting.

questions.question: What two concrete actions does the article ask readers to take?
questions.audience: candidates and campaign staff running on abolition
questions.answer: Share the article (and tag people in Senate District 13 who likely received McCortney's mailer), and tell anyone donating to National Right to Life or its state affiliates to cancel their donations.
questions.quote: 1. Share this article. And if you know anyone in Oklahoma Senate District 13 (Ada, Pauls Valley, Roff, Stratford, Fitzhugh, Allen, Sasakwa, Wynnewood, Elmore City, Byng, Paoli, Wanette, Konawa, Asher, Holdenville, and Maud), tag them in the post to make sure they see it.

questions.question: How does Silberman characterize the role pro-life leaders play in the abolition fight?
questions.audience: pro-life Christians weighing abolition vs. incrementalism
questions.answer: He claims they are the most consistent and fervent opponents of immediate abolition at every stage of the legislative fight.
questions.quote: At every turn, it is the pro-life leaders and organizations who are most fervently and consistently opposing the immediate abolition of abortion.

related.title: Senator McCortney Again Denies SB13 a Fair Hearing
related.reason: Direct background on McCortney's procedural killing of SB13 referenced throughout this piece.

related.title: Greg McCortney: Abortion Rights Man of the Year
related.reason: Companion polemic framing McCortney as the chief obstacle to Oklahoma abolition.

related.title: My Conversation With Senator Greg McCortney
related.reason: First-person account of engaging McCortney directly on his opposition to SB13.

related.title: Pro-Life Texas State Rep. Jeff Leach Delivers Death Sentence to 110,000 Preborn Human Beings
related.reason: Documents the Texas parallel Silberman cites of a pro-life chairman killing an abolition bill.

related.title: Supreme Court Idolatry at Texas Alliance for Life
related.reason: Critiques the same Texas Alliance for Life that later rewarded Leach, extending the establishment-cover thesis.

related.title: Anti-SB13 Campaigners Aren't Ignorant. They're Trying to Deceive You.
related.reason: Argues that opposition to SB13 by pro-life groups is deliberate misinformation, the same dynamic this article alleges.

related.title: Pastor and Gubernatorial Candidate Dan Fisher Responds to Oklahoma Baptist Leaders Opposing SB13
related.reason: Another front in the SB13 fight showing institutional pro-life resistance vs. abolitionist demand.
